messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Build a dashboard module with auth and an API, with unit tests and database stubs. Include modules A and B.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Build a dashboard module with auth and an API, with unit tests and database stubs. Include modules A and B.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "requirements-and-architecture-design",
      "title": "Requirements and architecture design",
      "description": "Gather functional requirements for dashboard, modules A and B, auth, API, testing and stubbing; produce architecture diagrams and interfaces.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A documented requirements list and architecture diagram that defines module boundaries, API endpoints, auth flows, and data-access contracts accepted by stakeholders."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-scaffold-and-ci-setup",
      "title": "Project scaffold and CI setup",
      "description": "Create repository structure, package config, build scripts, and continuous integration pipeline for linting, building and running tests.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "requirements-and-architecture-design"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Repository with standard folder layout, build scripts, and a CI configuration that triggers on commits and runs lint and test steps successfully."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-module-a",
      "title": "Implement Module A",
      "description": "Develop module A core components and interfaces per design, including UI hooks if applicable and clear public methods for integration.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-scaffold-and-ci-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "Module A compiles and exposes documented interfaces; basic manual smoke tests pass against its public API."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-module-b",
      "title": "Implement Module B",
      "description": "Develop module B functionality and interfaces consistent with architecture; ensure it can interoperate with module A where required.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-scaffold-and-ci-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Module B compiles and its API is documented; integration points with Module A validated with simple calls."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-authentication",
      "title": "Implement authentication and authorization",
      "description": "Add auth layer (login, session/jwt handling, role checks) and integrate with dashboard access control.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-scaffold-and-ci-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Auth endpoints implemented and enforceable; authenticated sessions/tokens allow/deny access as per defined roles in acceptance tests."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-database-stubs",
      "title": "Create database stubs and data-access contracts",
      "description": "Implement in-memory or mock database stubs that satisfy data-access interfaces for modules and the API to use during testing.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-scaffold-and-ci-setup",
        "requirements-and-architecture-design"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Stub implementations return predictable test data and conform to data-access contracts; tests can run against stubs reliably."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-api-endpoints",
      "title": "Implement API endpoints for dashboard",
      "description": "Develop REST/GraphQL API endpoints that expose dashboard features, integrate modules A and B, enforce auth, and use database stubs.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-module-a",
        "implement-module-b",
        "implement-authentication",
        "create-database-stubs"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "API endpoints are implemented with documented routes/schema, pass contract tests, enforce auth, and operate correctly against stubs."
    },
    {
      "id": "write-unit-and-integration-tests",
      "title": "Write unit and integration tests",
      "description": "Author unit tests for modules A/B, auth, API handlers, and integration tests using database stubs to validate end-to-end flows.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-module-a",
        "implement-module-b",
        "implement-authentication",
        "create-database-stubs",
        "implement-api-endpoints"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Automated test suite covers key functionality (target coverage threshold defined), runs in CI, and all tests pass against stubs and mocked environments."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 8,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down modular decomposition: define requirements and architecture, scaffold the project, implement each module independently (A and B), add shared concerns (auth, DB stubs), then implement the API that composes modules and finally write tests to validate functionality. Prioritize clear interfaces and testability."
}
